summary: Iteration 143: Audit trail 100% coverage tests. All 20/20 backend tests pass. Every write endpoint tested verifies that a new entry is written to both audit_logs AND audit_trail collections immediately after the action. Tested: tax settings update, tax override create/delete, customer tax-exempt patch, invoice settings update, invoice template create/delete, integration request submit/status-update/note-add, file upload. Also bonus-tested tax table update + audit schema integrity.
critical_code_review_comments: uploads.py mixes sync MongoDB client (_db() uses pymongo.MongoClient) with async audit call (await create_audit_log). Works correctly but is inconsistent — the DB insert for file_uploads is synchronous while the audit log write is async. Low risk but worth noting for future refactor.

audit_service.create_audit_log() does NOT pop _id from the audit_logs document after insert_one — the _id field remains in MongoDB but is never returned to callers (inserts don't return the doc). No client-facing bug, but differs from the pattern used elsewhere.

integration_requests.py update_request_status() calls create_audit_log without a tenant_id kwarg — it relies on the _current_tenant_id ContextVar set by get_tenant_admin. This works for partner-admin-scoped calls, but for platform-admin calls (where there's no tenant_id set in context), the audit log will have tenant_id=None. Acceptable but worth noting.
All create_audit_log calls have correct entity_type, action, actor, and details fields. Dual-write to audit_logs + audit_trail is confirmed working.

verified_endpoints.PATCH /api/admin/customers/{id}/tax-exem: PASS - audit_log entity_type=customer action=tax_exempt_updated, details.tax_exempt verified
verified_endpoints.PUT /api/admin/taxes/invoice-settings: PASS - audit_log entity_type=invoice_settings action=updated, updated_fields in details
verified_endpoints.POST /api/admin/taxes/invoice-templates: PASS - audit_log entity_type=invoice_template action=created, name in details
verified_endpoints.PUT /api/integration-requests/{id}/statu: PASS - audit_log entity_type=integration_request action=status_updated, new_status in details
verified_endpoints.POST /api/integration-requests/{id}/note: PASS - audit_log entity_type=integration_request action=note_added, note_text in details
verified_endpoints.POST /api/uploads: PASS - audit_log entity_type=file_upload action=uploaded, filename+size in details
verified_endpoints.audit_trail dual-write: PASS - TaxSettings, InvoiceSettings, FileUpload entries all confirmed in audit_trail collection
verified_endpoints.schema integrity: PASS - all logs have id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor, created_at

seed_data_creation: Created test data during run: tax override rules (TEST_Audit_Override_*), invoice templates (TEST_Audit_Template_*), integration requests (TEST_Audit_Integration_*), and file uploads (test_audit.txt, audit_trail_test.txt). All test data cleaned up by delete tests where applicable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Audit trail 100% coverage feature is fully implemented and tested. All 11 target write endpoints confirmed creating correct audit_logs entries. The dual-write to audit_trail is also confirmed. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter143_audit_trail_coverage.py (20 tests, all pass). Partner admin test user from iter142 (test.integration.partner@iter142.test / TestIntegReq142!) was reused successfully. Note: uploads.py uses a sync pymongo client for file_uploads collection but async for audit - works but inconsistent.
